title: Electric Power Steering (EPS) Structure Description - Steering Column

blocks.text: Electric Power Steering (EPS) Structure Description - Steering Column

The steering force from the steering wheel is transmitted to the column shaft. The torque sensor measures the difference between the steering force transmitted to the column shaft and the resistance force that causes the wheels to rotate due to friction with the road surface, converts it into a voltage signal, and sends it to the EPS control unit. Based on this signal, the EPS control unit controls the current to the EPS motor. The worm gear is meshed with the worm wheel gear, which is integrated with the column shaft, so the driving force of the motor is transmitted directly to the column shaft.
